title: recount3 Local Run Helpers
text: rc3helpers little package designed around making easier work local samples ways: Running recount-pump recount-unify; Copying output recount-unify directory structure allows importing local data recount3 R package. Checking something hasn’t happened *.fq.gz files download copying locations.

title: recount3 Local Run Helpers
text: can install development version rc3helpers GitHub :

title: recount3 Local Run Helpers
text: assumes ’ve already gotten everything setup running pump unify locally. can find instructions monorail-external README. realize rc3_run_pump_unify function call takes lot arguments. However, given ’m horrible writing shell scripts, typically single sample run local hardware, cloud like Langmead lab , saves lot typing give directory samples, besides locations.
code: # NOT RUN, for example only unify_output = rc3_run_pump_unify(   fasta = "path/to/fasta.fq.gz",   outputs = "path/to/outputs/dir",   monorail = "path/to/monorail/repo",   recount_pump = "path/to/recount-pump.XXX.sif",   recount_unify = "path/to/recount-unify.XXX.sif",   reference_path = "path/to/references",   reference = "hg38",   studyid = "studyid",   shortid = "shortid",   ncore = 2 )

title: recount3 Local Run Helpers
text: monorail argument, can either top level directory normally monorail-external, vector length 2, giving paths shell scripts orchestrating recount-pump recount-unify respectively. useful working custom organism outside human mouse. example, working zebrafish, created slightly modified script handles recount-unify step properly zebrafish, define monorail argument like , unify step calls correct shell script:

title: recount3 Local Run Helpers
text: script currently expects sample two fasta files named: sample_id_1.fq.gz sample_id_2.fq.gz monorail-external also assumes last two places identifier sample_id, study_id shortid contain underscore non-alphanumeric character, .e. contain [-z] [0-9].

title: recount3 Local Run Helpers
text: Two important notes: recount3 v 1.22.0, option organism outside human mouse, always use directory one two organisms. also means certain annotations / references supported. Therefore, create symbolic links files recount3 expects . previously ran two human SRA samples local mode pump unify workflow, short_id sratest. directories created look like : unify output looks like : model organism, going need reference sets things downloaded . can download gene exon sum annotations recount3, case G026. can pass list directories created rc3_setup_directories rc3_copy_unify_outputs: create directory structure. may see error working human data, function tries link file actually already exists. ’s OK. can load recount3 start working dataset:
code: library(rc3helpers) tmp_dir = tempdir() rc3_dirs = rc3_setup_directory(base_dir = tmp_dir, short_id = "sratest") #> ℹ Using "/tmp/Rtmpa6E9DU" to create underlying folders ... #> ℹ Creating directories: "/tmp/Rtmpa6E9DU/human/data_sources/sratest/base_sums", "/tmp/Rtmpa6E9DU/human/data_sources/sratest/exon_sums", "/tmp/Rtmpa6E9DU/human/data_sources/sratest/gene_sums", "/tmp/Rtmpa6E9DU/human/data_sources/sratest/junctions", "/tmp/Rtmpa6E9DU/human/data_sources/sratest/metadata", "/tmp/Rtmpa6E9DU/human/annotations/gene_sums", and gene_sums         ├── junctions         └── metadata ├── exon_sums_per_study │   └── st │       └── sratest │           ├── sratest.exon_sums.sratest.ERCC.gz │           ├── sratest.exon_sums.sratest.SIRV.gz ├── gene_sums_per_study │   └── st │       └── sratest │           ├── sratest.gene_sums.sratest.ERCC.gz │           ├── sratest.gene_sums.sratest.F006.gz │ title: recount3 Local Run Helpers
text: Sometimes, issue filesystem, disks data stored, copy move file, something can go wrong file. recount-pump depends decompressing fasta files docker container run, anything gets corrupted fq.gz file, just won’t run. , assuming gzip installed system, can check either entire directory particular files running recount-pump unify. returns data.frame, error messages emitted gzip file. Ideally, see “NA” every entry running pump unify steps.

title: check gz files — rc3_check_gz
text: Runs gzip -t set files verify nothing wrong running pump unify. gzip must installed available system. potentially making lot system2 calls via purrr, easy interrupt begun.

title: check gz files — rc3_check_gz
text: fasta directory check, list files directly.

title: check gzip files — rc3_check_gzip
text: Runs gzip -t set files verify nothing wrong running pump unify. gzip must installed available system. potentially making lot system2 calls via purrr, easy interrupt begun.

title: check gzip files — rc3_check_gzip
text: fasta directory check, list files directly.

title: Copy Unify Output — rc3_copy_unify_outputs
text: Copy output generated unify script, setup directory, symlink things. Note recount3 expecting human mouse, relevant annotation. using , generated anything wanted (build handle zebrafish using MZ11). Therefore, copies files, symlinks names recount3 expects.

title: Copy Unify Output — rc3_copy_unify_outputs
code: rc3_copy_unify_outputs(   rc3_directory = NULL,   short_id = "test",   unify_directory = NULL,   references_directory = NULL,   organism = "human",   annotation = "G026" )

title: Copy Unify Output — rc3_copy_unify_outputs
text: rc3_directory want things copied (sort ) short_id short id project unify_directory unify outputs stored references_directory reference data stored organism organism data (real pretend) annotation annotations interested (real pretend)

title: Copy Unify Output — rc3_copy_unify_outputs
text: output "tree" command rc3_directory

title: run recount-pump and recount-unify — rc3_run_pump_unify
text: Given parameters, attempts create run commands carry runs recount-pump recount-unify set samples.

title: run recount-pump and recount-unify — rc3_run_pump_unify
code: rc3_run_pump_unify(   fasta = getwd(),   outputs = fs::path_dir(fs::path_expand(fasta)),   monorail = "monorail-external",   recount_pump = "recount-pump_1.1.3.sif",   recount_unify = "recount-pump_1.1.3.sif",   reference_path = ".",   reference = "hg38",   studyid = "other1",   shortid = "test",   ncore = 1 )

title: run recount-pump and recount-unify — rc3_run_pump_unify
text: fasta directory input samples run outputs keep outputs, assumed parent fasta monorail monorail repository recount_pump recount-pump sif image recount_unify recount-unify sif image studyid study id shortid short id ncore many cores use

title: run recount-pump and recount-unify — rc3_run_pump_unify
text: unify output directory

title: setup a new directory — rc3_setup_directory
text: Sets directory structure needed recount3 read data . Note recount3 currently works human mouse, regardless actual organism used, must choose one two make work.

title: setup a new directory — rc3_setup_directory
text: base_dir top level directory projects live short_id short identifier used unifying outputs

title: setup a new directory — rc3_setup_directory
text: project directory list

title: rc3helpers 0.0.3
text: Suppresses output going stdout. Handles fq.gz fastq.gz files.
